paragraphs: Sara Jenkins is a multimedia artist who focuses on pattern work that explores organic forms. Originally from Albuquerque, New Mexico, she currently resides in Brooklyn, New York. At a young age, Sara was taught to draw by her grandpa who was a self-taught artist. Her grandma later hired a friend, who was a medical textbook artist, to further her education. She holds a BA in studio art and creative writing from the University of New Mexico where she received the Toby Wright Scholarship. Her background includes participating in the Albuquerque Museum's Lead with the Arts program where she collaborated with Meow Wolf, working as a summer art teacher and private art tutor, setting up large concerts as a stagehand, and displaying her work in multiple group shows. In her spare time, she walks in natural areas where she studies plants.
